text: 0.2g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, the cleanest win is a more stable carb profile.
26.5g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
1.5g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
everything else is kind of whatever.
potassium at 13% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

pros first: easy comfort-food base with less baggage than junk food because that practical swap value matters. whole-food carb package because that matters because it is a cleaner baseline than heavily refined carbs. practical staple carb source because tubers are often judged by how usable they are day to day.

the drawbacks next: protein support is weak because tubers still need help from the rest of the meal. can go downhill fast when heavily processed because the category has a big preparation trap. still a carb-heavy category because that is the core ceiling tubers have to manage.
A tier. Best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
